children.name: Certhia
children.description: Slender streak-backed birds that spiral up tree trunks like animated pieces of bark, probing crevices with a fine curved bill. They always work upward — once at the top they drop to the base of the next tree and begin again. The two Swedish species are almost identical and best separated by their songs.

children.name: Certhia familiaris
children.description: A streaky, bark-coloured bird that spirals upward around tree trunks probing crevices with a fine curved bill, then drops to the base of the next tree to start again. The commoner of Sweden's two near-identical treecreepers, best identified by song.

children.name: Certhia brachydactyla
children.description: Almost indistinguishable from the Eurasian Treecreeper except by its harder, more rhythmic song and habitat preferences, reaching the northern edge of its range in southernmost Sweden. It favours broadleaved woodland and parkland.
